objective:
Objectif de stratégie crypto : Génère une stratégie basée sur les familles robustes de signaux (breakout, momentum) et quelques combinaisons indicatrices innovantes (curated), avec un risque management non linéaire pour la volatilité. Utilise des filtres originaux pour l'asymetrie long/short (seuils differents) et une adaptation de régime (trend vs range). Évalue également les comportements aléatoires, comme un mode inverse ou un filtrage horaire-horodatage. Propose des hypothèses testables et falsifiables pour l'analyse des signaux de trading.

Pour ce faire, supposez une stratégie basée sur le marché 0GUSDC en 1h avec les indicateurs suivants : EMA (Exponential Moving Average) + OBV (On Balance Volume), BOLLINGER + STOCHASTIC, et EMA. Utilisez un SL/TP pour réguler la volatilité sur le marché 0GUSDC en fonction de l'adaptation du régime (trend vs range).

iterations.hypothesis: Fallback contractuel: proposition générée automatiquement pour maintenir la progression quand la sortie LLM n'est pas exploitable.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy aims to generate a robust and innovative trading signal based on multiple indicators such as EMA, OBV, BOLLINGER + STOCHASTIC, and trend/range adaptation for the 0GUSDC market in 1h. The strategy also includes fallback contract generation if LLM is not exploitable. Hypothesis testing will be done using RSI, EMA, and ATR indicators. Entry long occurs when a higher momentum is confirmed with controlled risk. Exit occurs on signal reversal or invalidation of the high-momentum signal. Potential weaknesses include overfitting and lack of robustness against market fluctuations. In case of poor results (negative return), adjustments could include trying different filters for asymmetry and volatility management, as well as modifying the adaption regime between trend and range. If results are mediocre but still profitable, parameter optimization may be considered to improve Sharpe ratio.
[Backup plan] None

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] Iteration 2/10 is currently running the backtest on market 0GUSDC in 1h timeframe. Hypothesis and strategy are outlined, with fallback contract to maintain progress when LLM cannot be exploited. The previous iteration resulted in Sharpe=-20.000, trades=246, return=-108.18%. If results are poor (negative return), the potential weaknesses of this strategy would need to be identified and addressed. This could include re-evaluating certain indicators or adapting risk management techniques. For a mediocre Sharpe ratio, adjustments may be made to improve performance such as fine tuning SL/TP values or exploring different combinations of indicators. Lastly, an alternative approach would be considered if the current one fails.
[Backup plan] Backup Plan: 
	1. If results are poor (negative return), re-evaluate strategy and ensure that all components are working effectively together. Identify any weaknesses in the hypothesis and adjust accordingly. 
	2. For a mediocre Sharpe ratio, consider adjustments such as fine tuning SL/TP values or exploring different combinations of indicators to improve performance.
	3. If results fail after these modifications, consider alternative approaches within the crypto market or explore other markets for more promising strategies.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy utilizes a combination of EMA, Bollinger Bands, Donchian Channel and ATR for trend detection along with Stochastic RSI for momentum trading. The entry long condition is when close price crosses above the Bollinger Middle band while stochastic RSI is in buying zone. Exit conditions are when 3*ATR from entry or close Price crosses above Donchian Channel Upper Band. Hypotheses have been made based on historical market data, but further testing and validation is needed for robustness.
[Backup plan] 1) Possible weaknesses include the use of Bollinger Bands which may not be as effective in a highly volatile market like 0GUSDC. Adjustments could involve using different volatility indicators or adding filters to exclude high-volatility periods.2) If results are poor, one potential solution is to refine the entry and exit conditions by incorporating additional technical indicators or filtering techniques that better reflect favorable market conditions.3) To increase Sharpe ratio, tweaking parameters like period lengths for EMA and ATR can be considered as well as adjusting the risk/reward ratios in favor of higher returns.
